title: pittosporum umbellatum
intro: Pittosporum umbellatum, also known as Kohuhu or New Zealand tobacco tree, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to relieve coughs and colds. The leaves and stems of the Pittosporum umbellatum can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
